title: dracocephalum fruticulosum subsp. psammophilum
intro: Dracocephalum fruticulosum subsp. psammophilum, a lesser-known subspecies of the Dracocephalum fruticulosum plant, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to Central Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dracocephalum fruticulosum subsp. psammophilum include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are traditionally used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
